cardTitle: AI and Algorithms Qualification Toolkit (AI AQT)
description1: AI AQT provides a well-founded assessment whether a data-driven application falls under the AI Act and/or the GDPR, including the associated roles and responsibilities, through dynamic questionnaires.
description6: These dynamic questionnaires are developed for public sector organizations but can also be valuable for other sectors.
feedback: Help develop and share feedback via
save output: Save the outcome for future reference:
error message: An error occurred. No output was found.
disclaimer: Note: Always verify the outcomes of this tool using legal expertise, the legal text and additional relevant sources. This tool contains interpretations of complex legal definitions, as understood by Stichting Algorithm Audit. The interpretation of these definitions has not yet crystallized and many more years, additional guidance and various court cases will be needed before there is a shared universal interpretation. In addition, nuance is sometimes lost when translating legal definitions into understandable language. No rights can be derived from the outcomes of this tool.
summary intro: Based on your answers, the following regulatory frameworks are applicable:
summary none: Based on your answers, no policy frameworks are applicable.
summary next steps: The table below outlines the next steps you should take:
next step ai: Complete the Risk category questionnaire to determine which AI Act requirements apply.
next step algo: Register the algorithm in the Algorithm Register and verify internal algorithm policy.
next step gdpr: Comply with the data protection requirements of the GDPR.
next step sadm: Consult legal experts to verify whether sADM under Art. 22 GDPR applies.
next step profiling: Comply with profiling requirements under Art. 4(4) GDPR.
article gdpr art22 label: Art. 22 GDPR - Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
intro text: By answering approximately 10 questions in this questionnaire, it is determined which laws and regulations your algorithmic application falls under.
intro next steps note: Based on the results of this questionnaire, next steps are provided to support legal compliance.
intro concepts label: The following concepts and policy frameworks are identified:
intro ai note: Based on analysis of uploaded documents, questions may have been pre-filled by AI. Verify whether the suggested answers are correct.
meta data ai note tooltip: AI will assess whether questions from the compliance questionnaires can be answered and pre-filled with confidence, based on the user's input and uploaded documents. If the AI is uncertain about an answer, that question will be left blank. All pre-filled answers are clearly flagged for the user for verification.
aiact2 conclusion 113 text: Based on your answers, the AI Act does not apply to you, because you are not a provider, deployer, importer or distributor. However, carefully verify whether one of these roles might still apply to you.
aiact2 intro downstream provider prefix: A 'Downstream provider' equals a 'Provider'. Only in case an AI model is used, this distinction is relevant (see
aiact2 intro downstream provider suffix: ). Whether an AI model is used, is determined in questionnaire Risk category.
aiact2 intro guidance bullets: A 'Product manufacturer' equals a 'Provider'. Being a product manufacturer is only relevant if: (i) the high-risk AI system relates to products for which the laws in Annex I Section A apply; (ii) the system is placed on the market or put into service together with the product; and (iii) under the name or trademark of the product manufacturer. In this case the product manufacturer is considered to be the provider of the high-risk AI system (see [Art. 25(3) AI Act](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-25)).
aiact2 intro p1 prefix: This questionnaire is only applicable if the questionnaire
aiact2 intro p1 suffix: has determined that an AI system is applicable.
aiact2 intro p2 prefix: Based on 2-3 questions, it is determined what role you have with respect to the AI system. Possible roles and status are:
aiact2 intro role deployer desc: Natural or legal person using an AI system under its own authority in a professional context (Art. 3(4) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role distributor desc: Natural or legal person in the supply chain that makes an AI system available on the Union market without altering its properties (Art. 3(7) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role importer desc: Natural or legal person established in the Union who places on the market an AI system bearing the name or trademark of a non-EU entity (Art. 3(6) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role private desc: AI system is used in the course of a personal, non-professional activity (Art. 3(4) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role provider desc: Natural or legal person that develops an AI system or general-purpose AI model and places it on the market under its own name or trademark (Art. 3(3) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role representative desc: A natural or legal person located or established in the Union who has received and accepted a written mandate from a provider of an AI system to carry out on its behalf the obligations and procedures established by the AI Act (Art. 3(5) AI Act).
aiact2 intro status in development desc: New AI systems face shorter deadlines depending on the type of AI system (Art. 113 AI Act).
aiact2 intro status in use desc: AI systems already in use before the AI Act entered into force must comply by 2030 at the latest with requirements of the AI Act (Art. 111 AI Act).
aiact2 q12 a1: We only develop the AI system.
aiact2 q12 a10: You are a natural person using an AI system for purely personal non-professional activity.
aiact2 q12 a11: We represent a non-EU person or entity to carry out AI Act obligations.
aiact2 q12 a2: We develop the AI system and others also use it.
aiact2 q12 a3: We develop and use the AI system ourselves.
aiact2 q12 a6: We use an externally developed AI system.
aiact2 q12 a7: We import the AI system from a supplier outside the EU.
aiact2 q12 a8: We buy the AI system from a supplier within the EU and resell it.
aiact2 q12 title: How is the AI system developed or deployed?
aiact2 q12b m1: The name or trademark of your organization is put on the AI system.
aiact2 q12b m2: A substantial modification has been made to the AI system.
aiact2 q12b m2 tooltip: A substantial modification to an AI system is a significant change to its architecture, training data or functionality that materially alters how it performs or the outputs it produces. When a high-risk AI system continues to learn after being placed on the market or put into service, this is not considered a substantial modification, provided the changes were pre-determined at the moment of the initial conformity assessment and described in the initial technical documentation (Article 43(4) AI Act).
aiact2 q12b m3: The intended purpose of the AI system is modified by your organization.
aiact2 q12b title: Does one of the following scenarios apply to your AI system?
aiact2 q12b user guidance: Whether a distributor, importer or deployer becomes a provider under the above scenarios mainly matters for high-risk AI systems. The Risk category questionnaire establishes the system's risk classification. In this questionnaire, it is determined whether a relevant a responsibilities along the AI value chain applies.
aiact2 q13 description: An AI system is considered 'in use' as soon as it is actually applied, including in a pilot or testing. A system is 'in use' if it has effect on work processes.
aiact2 q13 explanation intro: This matters because different compliance deadlines apply for AI Act requirements:
aiact2 q13 explanation item0: The AI Act itself doesn't use the phrase "in use". It uses "placed on the market" or "put into service" (as defined in [Art. 3(9)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-3) and [Art. 3(11)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-3)).
aiact2 q13 explanation item1:
AI systems already in use before the AI Act entered into force must comply by 2030 at the latest with requirements of the AI Act.
- A high-risk AI system must have been in use before 2 August 2026 ([Art. 111(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-111)).
- High-risk AI systems in use by public authorities must comply with the AI Act's requirements by 2 August 2030 ([Art. 111(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-111)).
aiact2 q13 explanation item2: New AI systems face shorter deadlines depending on the type of AI system.
aiact2 q13 title: Is the AI system already in use?
aiact2 result ai system status prefix: The status of the AI system is:
aiact2 result in development text: This means shorter compliance deadlines (before 2030) apply, depending on the type of application.
aiact2 result in use text: This means you must comply in 2030 at the latest with AI Act requirements.
aiact2 result role definition prefix: The legal definition of this term can be found in:
aiact2 result role definition prefix plu: The legal definitions of these terms can be found in:
aiact2 result role identified prefix: Based on your answers, you have been identified as a
aiact2 result role identified suffix: in relation to the AI system.
article art113 label: Art. 113 AI Act - Entry into force and application
article art25 label: Art. 25 AI Act - Responsibilities along the AI value chain
